rules.description: Every API endpoint must follow the security pipeline: validate HTTP method → rate limit → validate input/ID → validate structure → content filter → auth check → perform operation → handle errors. Missing any step is a security gap.
All endpoints must call checkRateLimit() with the correct action tier. CRUD: 100/min, report: 10/hr, suggest: 20/hr, slicer: 30/hr. Rate limit failures must return 429 with retryAfter.

Validate all input at the boundary: size limits (500KB layout, 100KB designer), bin count (2500 max), string lengths, custom property constraints. Use validators from api/lib/validation.ts and api/lib/designerValidation.ts.

User-generated content (names, labels, notes, categories) must pass through filterLayoutContent() from api/lib/contentFilter.ts before storage. Check for XSS, blocklisted terms, URL injection, spam patterns.
Delete tokens must be hashed with SHA-256 + TOKEN_SALT before storage. Token comparison must use timingSafeEqual (constant-time). Tokens go in Redis, never in public blob storage.
Use standardized error codes from api/lib/shared.ts (VALIDATION_ERROR, RATE_LIMITED, NOT_FOUND, etc.). Never leak internal details in error messages. Log with console.error for server errors only.
Redis operations must handle unavailability gracefully. Production: fail-closed (deny if Redis is down). Dev/preview: fail-open. Never block responses on cache write failures (fire-and-forget).
Custom property keys must be checked against reserved keys (__proto__, constructor, prototype, toString, valueOf, hasOwnProperty). Block prototype pollution vectors at the validation layer.
LLM endpoints must validate the Origin header against the allowlist (gridfinity.xyz, preview deploys, localhost). Requests from unlisted origins must be rejected before any LLM call is made.
